step_by_step_thinking:
The Vaccine Vial Monitor (VVM) is a self-indicating device placed on the label or vial cap that changes color when exposed to excessive temperature, indicating whether the vaccine has been stored outside its recommended range. This parameter directly assesses the impact of thermal exposure on vaccines and thus determines their sensitivity to heat. The correct option should be A: VVM. Analysis:

The Vaccine Vial Monitor (VVM) is specifically designed to indicate if a vaccine has been subjected to temperatures outside its safe storage range by changing color as it heats up over time. Therefore, this parameter accurately reflects the sensitivity of a vaccine to heat.
